title: UD Sentence Max-DD and Treebank Typological Features
summary: This dataset artifact extracts sentence-level maximum dependency distance (max-DD) data and treebank-level typological features from 238 qualifying Universal Dependencies treebanks (122 unique languages) via the commul/universal_dependencies HuggingFace dataset (UD v2.17, Parquet format). Sentence-level rows (420,049 total) capture max_DD, normalized max_DD, and non-projectivity flags for sentences of exact lengths n=10,12,14,16,18,20, filtered by qualifying thresholds (>=500 total sentences per treebank, >=50 per bin). Treebank-level rows (238 total) include morphological richness (range 0.0–4.5), head-direction ratio (0.18–0.79, spanning head-final Turkish to head-initial Guajajara), Levshina 2019 frequency-weighted word-order entropy (0.01–0.77), non-projectivity rate (0.0–0.62), feature annotation completeness, genre, and spoken/written modality labels cross-referenced against Dobrovoljc 2022. Dependency distance vectors and tree edge lists are stored in a separate supplementary_distances.json file (72MB) to keep the main output under the file size limit. The full dataset is split into two parts (72MB each) in data_out/. All outputs conform to the exp_sel_data_out.json schema with metadata_* fields for all typological and structural features needed for downstream GEV fitting and regression analysis.
